task_summary: Continue building Dupe Hunt from its current monorepo scaffold into a working community-powered dupe discovery platform with a Fastify API, Expo mobile app, and Next.js SEO web layer.

subtasks.title: Bootstrap the monorepo workspace
subtasks.description: Create the root pnpm/Turborepo TypeScript workspace so the documented repo structure exists and later iterations have real test, lint, and typecheck entry points.
acceptance_criteria: Root workspace files exist for pnpm and Turborepo orchestration.
The documented apps/ and packages/ directories are scaffolded with minimal manifests.
Root-level scripts for development and quality checks are defined and runnable.

subtasks.title: Establish shared config, types, and environment contracts
subtasks.description: Create the shared packages and base TypeScript, lint, and environment-validation patterns that the apps will consume.
acceptance_criteria: packages/config and packages/types exist with reusable exports.
Strict TypeScript configuration is shared across the workspace.
Environment example files or validation contracts exist for api, web, and mobile.

subtasks.title: Model the database package and local data infrastructure
subtasks.description: Implement packages/db with Drizzle schema, sequential migrations, seed data for categories, and local infrastructure configuration for PostgreSQL and Redis.
acceptance_criteria: packages/db contains schema definitions aligned to docs/technical.md.
Sequential migration files exist for the MVP tables and seed categories.
A documented local workflow exists to run migrations against development services.

subtasks.title: Scaffold the Fastify API application
subtasks.description: Create the API app with health checks, config loading, shared error handling, Redis/Postgres wiring, and a test harness that future endpoint work can extend.
acceptance_criteria: apps/api starts successfully and exposes a health endpoint.
Fastify plugins handle config, database access, and consistent error responses.
API tests can run in isolation with the chosen test framework.

subtasks.title: Implement auth, users, and categories API flows
subtasks.description: Add Supabase-backed auth, JWT protection, user profile routes, category listing, and user category preference management with integration coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Auth endpoints exist for register, login, logout, refresh, and OAuth callbacks.
User and category endpoints return real database-backed data with validation.
Integration tests cover success and failure paths for auth and user/category flows.

subtasks.title: Implement media upload, posts, and feed ranking APIs
subtasks.description: Build media upload signing, post CRUD, deterministic feed queries, and BullMQ worker wiring for video and affiliate background jobs.
acceptance_criteria: Authenticated users can obtain media upload URLs and create posts.
GET /posts and GET /posts/:id implement the documented filters, pagination, and ranking rules.
Integration tests cover post creation, deletion, and feed retrieval.

subtasks.title: Implement social, search, affiliate, and admin API surfaces
subtasks.description: Add upvotes, downvotes, saves, flags, follow flows, Typesense-backed search, affiliate redirect tracking, and internal moderation/admin endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: Social interaction endpoints update persisted state and trigger the documented worker jobs.
Search endpoints return filtered results and trending terms.
Affiliate redirect tracking and admin endpoints are covered by automated tests.

subtasks.title: Scaffold the Expo mobile application shell
subtasks.description: Create the mobile app foundation with navigation, auth state, React Query client setup, API client utilities, and shared screen/component folders that match repo conventions.
acceptance_criteria: apps/mobile boots with Expo and TypeScript configuration.
Navigation is centralized under apps/mobile/src/navigation.
Auth token storage and API client plumbing exist without embedding business logic in screens.

subtasks.title: Deliver mobile auth, onboarding, and feed experience
subtasks.description: Implement the initial user-facing mobile flow from welcome through category selection into the feed and dupe detail screens.
acceptance_criteria: Users can register or sign in, complete onboarding, and persist category preferences.
Feed tabs, category filters, infinite scrolling, and dupe detail navigation work against the API.
The mobile app handles loading, empty, and basic error states for the implemented screens.

subtasks.title: Deliver mobile post creation and profile/social screens
subtasks.description: Implement the camera/gallery post flow, preview and publish path, plus profile, saved items, and core social interactions in the mobile app.
acceptance_criteria: Users can capture or select media, complete the form, and publish a post.
Profile, saved collection, and public profile screens render real API data.
Optimistic UI exists for upvote, save, and follow interactions.
subtasks.progress_note: Iteration 8 shipped the live composer/profile/social screens, and iteration 9 finished native camera/gallery selection plus signed binary upload before post creation.

subtasks.title: Build the Next.js browse and SEO web layer
subtasks.description: Create the browse-only web experience with SSR category, post, user, and search pages, metadata generation, affiliate redirect handling, and responsive layouts.
acceptance_criteria: The web app renders the documented SEO routes with server-side data fetching.
Each public page provides metadata and supports social sharing previews.
Web users can browse posts and trigger affiliate redirects, but cannot create posts.

subtasks.title: Add receipt upload and verification submission API flow
subtasks.description: Implement the authenticated API slice for private receipt uploads and post-level verification submission so clients can kick off verified-buy checks without exposing receipt URLs.
acceptance_criteria: Authenticated users can request signed receipt upload targets for their own posts.
Submitting a receipt stores only a private storage reference and queues verification work.
API responses expose receipt verification status without returning private receipt URLs.

subtasks.title: Complete OCR-backed receipt resolution across API and clients
subtasks.description: Add worker-backed OCR receipt checks, persist verified/failed outcomes, and surface the resulting verification states in mobile and web experiences.
acceptance_criteria: Receipt verification jobs can mark posts as verified or failed based on OCR results.
User verified-buy counts and related profile/feed signals stay consistent after receipt decisions.
Mobile and web surfaces display the resulting receipt verification states correctly.

subtasks.title: Harden moderation, abuse limits, and launch UX safeguards
subtasks.description: Add the remaining moderation queue polish, abuse-throttling rules, and launch-quality UX protections needed for beta readiness.
acceptance_criteria: Moderation reviewers have the missing queue/workflow support needed beyond the current API endpoints.
Basic abuse limits protect posting, affiliate spam, and receipt misuse paths.
User-facing safeguards cover the key loading, empty, and failure cases for launch-critical flows.

subtasks.title: Add CI quality gates and beta release documentation
subtasks.description: Create automated CI checks for the workspace quality commands and document the beta-ready release path, privacy requirements, and monitoring hooks.
acceptance_criteria: CI runs the documented workspace quality commands on repository changes.
The repo documents the beta release path, including privacy-sensitive receipt handling.
Operational hooks for launch verification and monitoring are documented or scaffolded.

discovered_issues: The root workspace typecheck currently relies on apps/web/src/next-shim.d.ts because the monorepo's NodeNext TypeScript config does not resolve Next.js subpath declarations directly.
The root lint command is still a custom workspace invariant check rather than a full code-style linter such as ESLint or Biome.

The API now enqueues background work through an app-local job queue seam for post creation, but real BullMQ-backed worker execution is still deferred until the later worker-heavy iterations flesh out processing and caching infrastructure.

Mobile post uploads currently read the selected local asset into memory before PUT-ing it to the signed storage URL, which is acceptable for MVP photos and short clips but may need a streaming upload path for larger media later.

Receipt OCR failures now persist as a first-class post state, but notifying creators about failed verification attempts is still deferred until the later launch-safeguards work adds a real notification surface.
